data.short_description: Supper club-style restaurant with vintage-inspired design and mezzanine lounge. Menu features contemporary American dishes including steaks, pork chops, short ribs, and house-made desserts. Owned by Malachy Noone with partners Kyle Miller and Frederick Hancock.

A 2,700 sq ft restaurant with ~66 seats and hybrid counter/table service. The first North Carolina location for the brand, featuring brisket, pulled pork, spare ribs, smoked chicken, and Carolina vinegar-style barbecue. Owned by Abby Haywood and Evangeline Smith.

Speakeasy-style bourbon bar and cigar lounge with hidden entrance and upscale interior. Menu highlights include bourbon-focused cocktails, Neapolitan pizza, and small plates. Owned by Ralph Mensah and Michael Thomas.

Coffee shop, bookstore, and vinyl concept with rotating curated inventory and workspace vibe. Menu includes a coffee program plus curated books and records with a local focus. Owned by Jermaine Bantum.
Restaurant targeting a summer opening at 318 Blackwell St, Durham. Listed as being in early construction phase. Article text is truncated; no further details available.

Fast-casual barbecue restaurant with a drive-thru and limited indoor seating for about 12 guests, opening in the former Mr. K's BBQ space. Menu centers on pit-cooked barbecue including chicken, ribs, and classic sides. Owned by Robert Rukab and partner Zee.

data.location: 6290 N Atlantic Ave, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
data.short_description: Cozy, beach-inspired café with indoor seating and a patio near the cruise port. Menu features espresso drinks, baked goods, and the Dawn Patrol Latte. Owned by Sofia Ross and Brayden Montgomery.

data.location: 4285 N Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
data.short_description: Fast-casual smoothie shop connected to neighboring concepts. Menu includes no-ice smoothies, fresh juices, and açaí bowls. Owned by Ron Abeles.

data.short_description: Family-run coffee and ice cream shop with counter service. Menu includes ice cream, shakes, Italian sodas, and specialty coffee. Owned by Alyssa and Travis Froland.

data.location: 820 E New Haven Ave, Suite 3, Melbourne, FL 32901
data.short_description: Hybrid tea service concept with Victorian and boho design. Menu includes loose leaf teas, sandwiches, soups, and baked goods. Owned by Noah Nace.

Craft brewery targeting an April 1 opening in Southport, NC. Led by partners Alan A. McDonald, Kevin J. Wright, and Kevin Bower.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: New food truck concept focused on steak and frites, primarily serving hanger steaks over fries with sauces including chimichurri. Will be stationed at the same Ball Ground location as Cheez'd and Confuzed. Owned by Daniel Ray.

Second Potbelly location for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast in the Jacksonville market. Expected to have about 20 indoor seats plus a few patio tables, part of a seven-location Northeast Florida growth plan.
First Potbelly location for owners Patrick and Leah Pendergast in the Jacksonville market, debuting in April. Part of a larger seven-location Northeast Florida expansion plan.

Counter ordering via digital menu. Owned by Michelle Sorensen and George Stacy.

data.short_description: Takeout-focused Asian fusion concept with limited dine-in seating for about 20–30 guests and a drive-thru pickup lane for online orders. Menu features bento-style meals, ramen, noodle dishes, wings, soups, and seasonal selections. Owned by Yunzhong Chen.

Small fast-casual bagel shop near the south end of Siesta Key with limited indoor and shared outdoor courtyard seating. Menu features fresh daily New York-style bagels with house-made cream cheeses, salmon toppings, espresso drinks, and canned-to-order cold brew. Owned by restaurateur Robert DeSilvio.

Revival of a nostalgic Sarasota drive-in concept with counter ordering and kiosks supporting dine-in, takeout, delivery, and catering options. Menu features classic burgers, fries, onion rings, and milkshakes. Majority owned by Troy King with partner Dimitris Syros.

Full-service Japanese restaurant paired with a cocktail lounge inside a restored historic building, with initial seating for roughly 135 guests. Menu includes vegan sushi, tableside hibachi, filet-focused steak options, pineapple fried rice, and Korean short rib. Owned by Samuel Ray with partners Jeffrey Karasawa and Jason Klamson.

Slider concept operating from the brand's signature bright orange shipping-container "can" with patio seating. Menu features cheeseburger sliders, seasoned waffle fries, and shakes. Franchisee Rocky Moore and team.

Specialty grocery store paired with a counter-service café and full bar, including a private dining room. Menu includes house-made prepared foods, gluten-free breads and pastas, local meats and produce. Owned by Greg Lipman and Betty Bahl.

data.status: fully built out and awaiting final city approvals
data.short_description: Franchise Fat Shack location fully built out and awaiting final city approvals, with casual dine-in setup for about 10 tables and beer and wine service. Owner Videsh Kumar's second Fat Shack, following his Acworth location that opened in 2021.

Counter-service Italian deli and market spanning about 1,800 square feet offering hot and cold sandwiches, deli salads, desserts, grab-and-go meals, and beer and wine. Targeting a May opening as final equipment installation and setup continue. Owned by Brandon Brock.

Craft brewery combining two buildings into approximately 2,000 square feet indoors with an outdoor beer garden featuring a live music stage, on a quarter-acre property. Beer styles include lagers, IPAs, and session beers brewed on-site, with food truck partnerships planned. Led by partners Alan A. McDonald, Kevin J. Wright, and Kevin Bower.

data.event_date: Soft opening: Thursday (unspecified date); Grand opening: the 30th
data.status: soft opening planned for Thursday, with grand opening celebration and blessing scheduled for the 30th
data.short_description: Soul Food Flavor is a soul food restaurant from Bobby and Christy Pressley, previously focused on catering. The menu features breakfast items and hearty plate lunches and dinners, served Monday through Saturday. The grand opening is open to the public with community members and visitors from around Georgia expected to attend.

data.status: planned to open in late March 2026
data.short_description: Sunny's Cafe is a father-daughter owned independent coffee shop coming to West Vero Beach. The cafe will seat up to 18 guests and offer counter service with dine-in and take-out options. The menu will feature breakfast, lunch, specialty coffee drinks, and locally sourced ingredients.

Nori Nite is a sushi and Laotian fusion concept planned for Alachua, FL. Owner Oulay Inthalangsy has more than 10 years of sushi-making experience and is returning to the restaurant industry after seven years as a firefighter.

data.location: 1975 South Ocean Drive, Hallandale Beach, FL 33009
data.short_description: Patrizia's Waterfront is the brand's 18th location, expanding from its New York roots to Hallandale Beach. The restaurant will offer Italian-American cuisine with lunch, dinner, and weekend brunch service in a waterfront setting. An official opening date has not yet been announced.

data.location: 2448 N Heritage Oaks Path, Hernando, FL 34442
data.short_description: Palms of India will introduce authentic Indian cuisine to Citrus County, where no other Indian food options currently exist according to the article. Owner Jeffrey Singh confirmed the details to Business Debut.

data.location: 470 Marina Drive, Port St. Joe, FL 32456
data.short_description: Ralphie's Fish Camp is a family-friendly seafood restaurant planned at the Port St. Joe city marina. Owner Grant Rish is a sixth-generation Port St. Joe native.

data.location: 1975 South Ocean Drive, Hallandale Beach, FL 33009
data.short_description: Patrizia's Waterfront is the brand's 18th location bringing Italian-American cuisine to the Hemispheres Condominium community in Hallandale Beach. The menu includes housemade pastas, dishes prepared in a parmesan wheel, and a full bar program. An official opening date has not yet been announced.

data.location: 1410 S Federal Hwy, Dania Beach, FL 33004
data.status: targeting a second week of March opening
data.short_description: MOH Cafe is a Portuguese-inspired specialty coffee concept described by the owner as "a hybrid experience" centered on quality coffee, culture, and community. The menu includes Portuguese desserts, traditional sandwiches, salads, smoothies, and signature drinks, with all baked goods prepared in-house. Coffee will be imported from Portugal.

data.location: 3333 South Orange Avenue, Suite 105, Orlando, FL 32806
data.status: targeting a March 16 opening, pending final inspections
data.short_description: Sabai Thai Street Food is a family-owned, 32-seat casual full-service restaurant built around recipes from owner Billy Spradlin's wife's family in Thailand. The kitchen is led by his wife Viradee Pisara and son Alex Pisara, both chefs. Featured dishes include Tom Yum noodle soup and handmade Thai sausage from a multi-generational family recipe.

data.location: 11204 E M.L.K JR BLVD, Seffner, FL 33584
data.short_description: Bellies Kitchen is a Southern comfort food restaurant from first-time owner Shelby Lovely, operated as a family business. The soft opening on March 1 will feature free food, bounce houses, and family-friendly activities, with seating for 68 guests. The menu focuses on Southern staples such as chicken and waffles, fried chicken, half-pound burgers, and breakfast skillets.

Yonutz is a viral dessert concept known for its SMASHED donut — a donut sliced, filled with ice cream, pressed, and finished with toppings. The Miramar location will also feature Yo Clawz, an in-store claw machine arcade with eight or more machines. The location will be owned by franchisee Rashid Elghoul, a Miramar resident who will also serve as an area developer for South Florida.

data.location: 10108 SW Discovery Way, Port St. Lucie, FL 34987
data.status: soft opening around April 1 and a grand opening around April 20
data.short_description: Swift Grill is opening its second location as a hybrid concept combining a fast-casual side with build-your-own bowls, wraps, and pitas, and a separate tapas menu with bar service. The restaurant will seat approximately 121 guests and offer a full liquor menu including THC cocktails.

data.status: aiming for a June or July reopening
data.short_description: Kush Wynwood is targeting a return this summer after a two-year closure due to extensive building renovations on a nearly 100-year-old structure. The restaurant will reopen as a full-service concept with approximately 40 indoor seats, a full liquor license, and a menu centered on burgers, sandwiches, and salads.

data.location: 6901 Collins Ave, CU1, Miami Beach, FL 33141
data.status: part of a broader expansion plan, with several additional openings expected in the near term
data.short_description: Crema Gourmet Espresso Bar is opening its 27th location in Miami Beach as a fast-casual European cafe with a full kitchen. The space will offer approximately 100 total seats and operate from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. seven days a week. The menu features more than 20 breakfast options alongside lunch, dinner, and an espresso program using Illy coffee.

data.status: targeting an opening in the second week of April 2026
data.short_description: Allegro Ma Non Troppo is a new full-service restaurant from Carlos Galan and his sister Mar Galan, featuring a simplified oven-driven menu with no fryer or flat top. The menu includes three appetizers, three main courses, three sides, and one dessert, with service blending full-service and fast-casual elements.

Jatski's Ice Cream is a scratch-made ice cream shop where owners Amy and Bill Laakkonen will manufacture ice cream on-site. The 1,200-square-foot space will seat up to 12 guests and serve flavors such as honey vanilla, cuckoo's coconut, chocolate velvet, and rum raisin, along with sundaes, milkshakes, and pup cups.

data.event_date: Week of April 20, expected by end of April
data.status: opening expected by the end of April
data.short_description: Hopkins' Eatery is opening its fourth Tallahassee location, a locally owned sandwich and salad shop with more than 40 years in the city. This location will be the first with bar seating and alcohol service, and will include a grill allowing for expanded hot menu items.

data.location: 560 W Sample Road, Pompano Beach, FL 33064
data.event_date: Within the next month (from article date)
data.status: preparing to open within the next month
data.short_description: Betty's Cuisine is opening its first location inside AMP Solo Gas Station in Pompano Beach, FL. Owners Chris Destine and Betty Moreau are positioning it as the first step in a planned franchise model.

data.status: targeting a Memorial Day weekend opening
data.short_description: Sistas Place is a new southern soul food bistro from Nikki Lawson combining comfort food, indoor dining, and event hosting.

data.status: targeting an opening on or around August 1
data.short_description: Burrito Shak is planning its first Florida location in Cape Coral, owned by Antoinette Basso, Phil Basso, and Vito Basso. This will mark the brand's entry into the Florida market.

Sunny Mornings Cafe is a full-service sit-down restaurant offering a mix of Mexican and American breakfast dishes from scratch. The restaurant will seat approximately 80 guests, with a smaller indoor dining room and a larger outdoor patio.

data.status: targeting a late March opening, pending final permitting approvals
data.short_description: Sunny Mornings Cafe will offer a blend of authentic Mexican breakfast dishes and classic American favorites in a full-service, sit-down format. The family and friends locally owned concept will seat approximately 80 guests and operate 7 a.m. to 3 p.m. seven days a week. The menu includes chilaquiles, huevos rancheros, enchiladas, pancakes, omelets, and breakfast platters.
